channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Massive shark headed down Florida coast ahead of busy travel season
item.description: Florida researchers have identified a record-breaking great white shark as it is tracked down Florida's coastline ahead of spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks scores of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 17
item.description: At least 17 people have been killed in a monster storm sweeping across much of the U.S. The most fatalities as of Saturday afternoon were in Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Russ Bynum And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After Columbia arrests, international college students fall silent
item.description: In the span of a week, a hush has descended on higher education in the United States. International students and faculty have watched the growing crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters at Columbia University with apprehension.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Critics warn staff cuts at federal agencies overseeing US dams could put public safety at risk
item.description: Experts are warning that Trump administration workforce cuts at federal agencies overseeing U.S. dams are threatening their ability to provide reliable electricity, supply water to farmers and protect communities from floods.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These breakfast chains have raised prices the most, study finds
item.description: No, it's not just Americans at home being hit hard by rising egg prices. Diners at many popular breakfast chains are feeling the impact as well. A study from FinanceBuzz examined which fast-casual restaurants have raised prices the most over the last five years.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Education Department investigating dozens of universities after DEI warning letter
item.description: The Education Department announced Friday it is investigating more than 50 colleges and universities over what it called "racial preferences" in academics or scholarships, a move that comes amid the Trump administration's wider crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man describes cruelty during his two decades of captivity at his family home in Connecticut
item.description: A man rescued from a burning home in Connecticut told a shocking story of cruelty and constant hunger as he was held captive in a single room for 20 years by his father and stepmother, according to a newly released arrest warrant.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pete Buttigieg won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, leaving door wide open for 2028 run
item.description: SAUGATUCK, Mich. (AP) — Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced that he will not seek an open U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, a move that leaves the door wide open for him to seek a much bigger role as his party’s next presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Delphi murders: Full 'Bridge Guy' video releases on website supporting Richard Allen
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV) — A full, 43-second video taken by Libby German just before she and her friend Abby Williams were murdered has surfaced online. The video was a crucial piece of evidence in the Delphi murders trial, which ended in the conviction of suspect Richard Allen.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Columbia grad student's detention will stretch on as lawyers spar over Trump's plan to deport him
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Mahmoud Khalil will remain detained in Louisiana until at least next week but can finally speak to lawyers while they fight the Trump administration’s plans to deport the Columbia University graduate student for his role in campus protests against Israel, a judge decided at a hearing
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested in Utah nursing home may not be extradited for 1977 Hawaii murder
item.description: A man who was arrested at a Utah nursing home in connection to a 1970s Hawaii murder may be released, according to a proposed court motion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks parts of Trump's executive order seeking to punish law firm Perkins Coie
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge blocked President Donald Trump's administration Wednesday from enforcing portions of an executive order designed to punish a prominent law firm linked to Democratic-funded opposition research during the 2016 presidential campaign into ties between the Republican can
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, Fox 5

item.title: Search ends for Dane Paulsen after volunteer diver recovers body
item.description: Dane Paulsen, a 2-year-old boy, was found dead in an area near his family home in Siletz, Ore., after a massive search effort spanning weeks.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Iacobazzi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Study shows rain-soaking atmospheric rivers are getting bigger, wetter and more frequent
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As extreme weather events have hit the world hard in recent years, one meteorology term — atmospheric rivers — has made the leap from scientific circles to common language, particularly in places that have been hit by them. That stands to reason.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, ABC 4

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bags fly free no more: Southwest Airlines to end free checked luggage policy
item.description: Southwest Airlines will no longer allow bags to fly free. The airline announced Tuesday it will begin charging some customers who check baggage in May.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, Fox 5
